title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan targets the top-level validation of CCF (Central Control Fabric) power management (PM) flows within the SOC. It covers coordinated power state transitions, resource arbitration, stress detection and response, license handshake and arbitration, policy-driven flow control, and system-level observability and debug. CCF integrates with PMA, Punit, and core modules to orchestrate power state changes (C-states, frequency/voltage scaling), manage exclusive access to shared resources (MLC flush, IOSF channels), and enforce system policies. Key interactions include cross-module synchronization, telemetry-driven stress handling, license negotiation, and debug visibility, ensuring robust and safe PM flow operation under normal and stress conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan encompasses validation of: (1) coordinated state transitions and resource arbitration across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios; (2) telemetry and AI-based stress detection and response mechanisms for electrical, thermal, and power virus events; (3) license handshake and arbitration flows between PMA, Punit, and cores; (4) policy-driven flow control and ownership prioritization; (5) system-level observability and debug mechanisms for PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. The plan addresses normal operation, stress scenarios, contention, race conditions, and system recovery. It does not cover lower-level signal integrity or individual IP-specific PM flows outside CCF orchestration.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: All features require access to SOC-level debug and telemetry infrastructure, programmable stress injection capabilities, and synchronized PMA/Punit/core interfaces. Test environment must support controlled stress event generation, license arbitration triggers, and policy configuration. Observability tools must be available for real-time status monitoring and debug.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Potential security implications include unauthorized PM flow initiation, license spoofing, and denial-of-service via resource contention. Mitigations involve policy gating, license arbitration, and observability mechanisms to detect and prevent malicious or unintended transitions. Validation will ensure that security/performance policies are enforced and that flow ownership is correctly prioritized under stress and error conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of simultaneous stress events, false positives in stress detection, and loss of debug visibility under high contention. Contingencies involve fallback flows, enhanced observability, and additional stress scenario coverage to ensure robustness and survivability.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Prerequisites include validated PMA/Punit/core interfaces, functional telemetry and debug infrastructure, and programmable policy and license configuration. Stress injection tools and observability mechanisms must be operational prior to test execution.

title: System-Level Observability and Debug
subsection content: Validate that debug counters, status indications, and telemetry provide accurate, timely visibility into PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. Check for correct updates of status registers (e.g., SBFT_STATUS) and observability under simultaneous stress and error conditions, ensuring no loss of visibility or incorrect reporting.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Validate that SBFT status register and debug signals update correctly and remain observable under stress and error conditions.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Configure SBFT@Field, inject random break events and stress, randomize test patterns and work-point parameters, and sample status/debug signals throughout execution.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Randomly inject break events (thermal, throttle hammer, S1/S5 CPD), randomize test patterns and work-point parameters (voltage, frequency) within legal ranges.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Requires SBFT@Field test configuration, both cores enabled, MLC cluster active, and access to ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD, CORE_PMA_CR_IO_TAF_CFG_STATUS, and PMA_DEBUG4 signals.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Coverage includes all legal SBFT_STATUS transitions, all break event types, and all debug signal transitions under stress.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Involves MLC, PMA, SBFT@F, break event handling, and debug signal propagation; SBFT@F and static lockstep are mutually exclusive.

title: SBFT Status Register Update and Observability Under Stress and Error Conditions
subsection content: Debug signals must be observable, status updates only after both cores reach stage, throttle events disabled during SBFT@F, and break events update SBFT_LAST_BREAK_REASON.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Configure the module for SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled and MLC cluster active. Ensure ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS] is at reset value 0x0. During SBFT@F execution, inject random break events such as thermal, throttle hammer, and S1/S5 CPD. Randomize test patterns and work-point parameters (voltage, frequency) within legal ranges. Continuously sample ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS], CORE_PMA_CR_IO_TAF_CFG_STATUS, and all PMA_DEBUG4 debug signals throughout the test. Verify that SBFT_STATUS transitions only through legal values and that all debug signals reflect correct sequence and state transitions, even under simultaneous stress and error injection.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: 1. Configure module for SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled and MLC cluster active. 2. Set ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS] to reset value 0x0. 3. Start SBFT@F execution. 4. Randomly inject break events (thermal, throttle hammer, S1/S5 CPD) during test. 5. Randomize test patterns and work-point parameters (voltage, frequency) within legal ranges. 6. Continuously sample ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS] at 0x121, CORE_PMA_CR_IO_TAF_CFG_STATUS at 0x8198, and all PMA_DEBUG4 debug signals. 7. Log all status and debug signal transitions. 8. After test completion, analyze logs for correctness.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Pass if SBFT_STATUS transitions only through legal values (00, 01, 10), never 11, and all transitions are observable. Debug signals must reflect correct sequence and state transitions. Fail if any missed update, illegal value, or loss of visibility is detected.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Use logs from register reads and debug signal captures to confirm all transitions and observability.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for voltage and frequency changes during stress.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: Use OSBV/SVOS with latest supported version, PMON for register sampling, Perspec path for scenario automation, PythonSV for scripting, and Supercollider for concurrency stress. Map OS-level utilities for register checks and stress pattern injection.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with SBFT@Field support, correct BIOS knobs for enabling both cores and MLC cluster, OS kernel with default scheduler, and required drivers for debug signal access.

title: SBFT Status Register Update Under Randomized Stress
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: SoC at supported stepping and TDP, dual-core configuration, MLC cluster enabled, DIMMs and PCIe devices as per platform topology, thermal solution supporting stress events, debug instrumentation for PMA_DEBUG4, and power measurement setup.

title: SBFT Status Register Update with Simultaneous Core Progression
subsection content: With SBFT@Field test running, ensure only one core initiates SBFT@F start command. Progress both cores through the test, ensuring that SBFT_STATUS updates only after both cores reach the required stage. Monitor MLSBFTStatusXnnnH and MLSBFTStatusValidXnnnH signals from MLC to PMA. Sample status and debug signals at each core progression point to verify correct update timing and legal value transitions.

title: SBFT Status Register Update with Simultaneous Core Progression
subsection content: 1. Configure SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled. 2. Start SBFT@F with only one core initiating the start command. 3. Progress both cores through test stages. 4. At each stage, sample ML4_CR_SBFT_AT_FIELD[SBFT_STATUS], MLSBFTStatusXnnnH, and MLSBFTStatusValidXnnnH. 5. Log all transitions and verify that updates occur only after both cores reach the required stage.

title: SBFT Status Register Update with Simultaneous Core Progression
subsection content: Pass if SBFT_STATUS updates only after both cores reach the stage and transitions through legal values. Fail if update occurs prematurely, with illegal value, or is missed.

title: SBFT Status Register Update with Simultaneous Core Progression
subsection content: Use register and signal logs to confirm update timing and correctness.

title: SBFT Status Register Update with Simultaneous Core Progression
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. PVT checkers for voltage/frequency stability.

Verify that break events are correctly reflected in the register and that all debug signals remain observable and accurate during and after the event.

title: SBFT Status Register and Debug Signal Observability During Break Event Injection
subsection content: 1. Start SBFT@Field test with both cores enabled. 2. Inject break events (thermal, S1/S5 CPD) at random points during execution. 3. Sample CORE_PMA_CR_IO_TAF_CFG_STATUS[31:24] for SBFT_LAST_BREAK_REASON after each event. 4. Monitor all PMA_DEBUG4 debug signals for correct sequence and state transitions. 5. Log all register and debug signal values.

title: SBFT Status Register and Debug Signal Observability During Break Event Injection
subsection content: Pass if SBFT_LAST_BREAK_REASON updates correctly for each break event and all debug signals remain accurate and observable. Fail if any missed update, incorrect value, or loss of visibility.
